title: API gateway p99 spike (memory pressure + noisy neighbor CPU bursts)
narrative: p99 alert on api-gateway. Some requests stall ~700ms. Traffic slightly up. A batch job started on the same node.

alerts.name: cpu steal
alerts.summary: CPU steal time elevated on one core

groundTruth.mechanism: two slow paths: (1) major faults during cache misses under memory pressure (2) scheduling delays during batch CPU bursts
notes: A batch job is CPU-intensive and causes runqueue contention bursts.
Memory is near limit; swapping occurs occasionally.
Tail requests correlate with either (a) pgmajfault spikes or (b) high run queue + context switches.

terminal.help:
Try: top -bn1 | vmstat 1 5 | free -m | cat /proc/vmstat | grep -E pgmajfault /proc/vmstat
Focus: distinguish queueing (CPU) vs paging (memory).

grep.error: grep is only allowed on /proc/vmstat or /proc/loadavg

commandLibrary.help:
Allowed: top -bn1 | free -m | vmstat 1 5 | cat /proc/vmstat | cat /proc/loadavg | grep -E <pat> <file>
No pipes, redirects, or quotes.
